title: Webhook Idempotency
summary: Extend a payment webhook handler so duplicate and out-of-order events do not corrupt payment state.

evidence.note: A realistic financial backend extension emphasizing state, correctness, and edge cases.

rubric.label: Codebase comprehension
rubric.excellent: Explains current happy-path event application and state storage.

rubric.label: Problem framing
rubric.excellent: Clarifies duplicate event IDs, status precedence, unknown events, and audit needs.

rubric.label: AI direction
rubric.excellent: Uses AI to inspect transition edge cases but keeps state-machine decisions explicit.

rubric.label: Command of solution
rubric.excellent: Can defend why stale events are ignored and duplicates are safe.

rubric.label: MVP judgment
rubric.excellent: Adds idempotency and ordering without replacing the whole handler.

rubric.label: Correctness
rubric.excellent: Handles duplicate, stale, terminal, and missing-payment cases.

rubric.label: Testing and validation
rubric.excellent: Tests duplicate delivery and out-of-order status transitions.

rubric.label: Tradeoff reasoning
rubric.excellent: Names production persistence, replay, and audit-log limitations.

rubric.label: Communication and cadence
rubric.excellent: Explains event invariants clearly as they evolve.

checkpoints.title: Read the code
checkpoints.prompt: What state does the handler currently keep, and where does corruption happen?

checkpoints.title: Frame ambiguity
checkpoints.prompt: What transition and duplicate rules need clarification?

checkpoints.title: MVP plan
checkpoints.prompt: What is your state-machine invariant and test plan?

checkpoints.title: Risk check
checkpoints.prompt: What event sequence might still break your implementation?

checkpoints.title: Final explanation
checkpoints.prompt: Explain duplicate safety, stale-event handling, validation, and AI usage.
